concerns.text: The spec relies exclusively on the NIMS Creep Data Center dataset (US-01, FR-001) without defining a fallback mechanism. The upstream kickback explicitly flagged 'No Verified Source Found' for NIMS. The spec must define an alternative dataset (e.g., a specific subset of the Materials Project creep data, a different public repository, or a synthetic generation protocol) to ensure the research question is answerable if NIMS is inaccessible. Proceeding without this renders the project unexecutable.

The spec mandates a Wilcoxon signed-rank test (FR-005) for statistical significance, whereas the upstream Idea explicitly requested a 'paired t-test' (Expected Results section). While Wilcoxon is methodologically sound for non-normal distributions, this constitutes a drift in the defined evaluation metric. The spec must either justify this change as a necessary methodological correction (e.g., 't-test assumptions violated') or revert to the t-test to maintain fidelity to the original research question.

The spec requires '5-fold stratified by temperature quantiles' (US-02, FR-004), but the Idea specifies an '80% training / 20% test split stratified by temperature range' followed by '5-fold cross-validation on the training set'. The spec conflates the hold-out test strategy with the CV strategy. The spec must clarify if a separate hold-out test set is required (as per Idea) or if the nested CV is the sole evaluation method, ensuring the evaluation protocol matches the Idea's intent.
